name: kafka-stream-join-trigger
title: Join Kafka Streams
description: Subscribes to two or more Kafka topics and fires the flow when messages carrying the same joinKeyField value have been received from every configured topic within joinWindowMs milliseconds — a classic stream-join / stream-enrichment pattern. Each topic uses its own consumer group. When the window expires before all topics contribute, an optional timeout handler fires with the partial data.

display.description: Fire a flow when matching messages arrive from all configured Kafka topics within a time window.

display.name: Kafka Connection
display.description: TIBCO Kafka shared connection providing broker addresses, authentication, and TLS settings.

display.name: Topics
display.description: Comma-separated list of Kafka topics to join (minimum 2). Example: orders,payments — messages from both topics with the same joinKeyField value will be merged into a single flow invocation.

display.name: Consumer Group (Base)
display.description: Base Kafka consumer group ID. The trigger creates one consumer group per topic named '<base>-<topicName>' (e.g. my-join-cg-orders, my-join-cg-payments). Must be unique per trigger instance.

display.name: Join Key Field
display.description: Message field whose value is used to correlate messages across topics (e.g. order_id, device_id). A message from each topic must have the same value for this field within joinWindowMs for the join to fire.

display.name: Join Window (ms)
display.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for all topics to contribute a message with the same joinKeyField value. When the window expires before all topics contribute, a timeout event is emitted (if a timeout handler is configured).

display.name: Initial Offset
display.description: Where to start consuming when no committed offset exists for a consumer group.

display.name: Balance Strategy
display.description: Kafka consumer group rebalance strategy applied to all per-topic consumer groups. 'roundrobin' (default) | 'sticky' (minimises reassignments) | 'range'.

display.name: Commit Completing Offset on Success Only
display.description: When true (default), the completing (last-arriving) message's offset is marked only when all joined handlers complete without error — at-least-once for the completing topic. When false, the offset is always committed. Note: offsets for non-completing topics are always committed immediately when their contribution is recorded, as Sarama sessions cannot be held open across topic boundaries.

display.name: Handler Timeout (ms)
display.description: Maximum time in milliseconds allowed for all handlers to complete. 0 (default) means no timeout. When exceeded the handler is treated as failed and, if Commit Completing Offset on Success Only is true, the offset is not marked.

display.name: Join Store Type
display.description: Backing store for in-flight join state. 'memory' (default): process-local, no dependencies, state lost on restart. 'file': JSON snapshot on shutdown and rebalance, requires Persist Path.

display.name: Persist Path (file store)
display.description: Absolute file path for the JSON snapshot when Store Type is 'file'. Example: /var/data/flogo/join-state.json. The directory is created automatically.

display.name: Event Type
display.description: 'joined' (default): fires when all topics contribute a matching message within the window. 'timeout': fires when the window expires before all topics contribute (for DLQ / partial-result handling). 'all': fires for both joined and timeout events.
